issues.description: Questions lack accompanying text passages - cannot assess textual evidence without source material
Standard RI.6.1 requires citing evidence from specific texts, but no source texts are provided
Questions 9-10 use complex analytical language inappropriate for Grade 6 (e.g., 'central idea development', 'intended audience analysis')
Multiple choice questions reference specific paragraphs, quotes, and characters without providing the source text
Answer key format inconsistent between multiple choice (includes letter prefix) and constructed response (explanation only)
Vocabulary in some questions may be challenging for Grade 6 ('resilient', 'inference', 'protagonist's motivation')

strengths: Clear organizational structure with appropriate section headers
Good variety of question types targeting textual evidence skills
Questions 1-5 demonstrate appropriate multiple choice format
Objectives align well with RI.6.1 standard requirements
Content maintains neutral worldview as required
recommendations: Include 1-2 grade-appropriate text passages for students to reference
Simplify language in questions 9-10 for Grade 6 comprehension level
Ensure all questions reference specific line numbers or paragraphs from provided texts
Standardize answer key format across question types
Consider replacing advanced vocabulary with grade-level appropriate alternatives
